comment.body:
That’s not completely true. Last Tuesday on 2/18/25 at around 8:40AM you wrote:

“long ADI, AAPL, NVDA, AMD🟢 *Feb 28 expo”

This was when NVDA was trading at between $141.80-$143, it’s absolute high of the last 2 weeks. You updated saying you took profit on your ADI trade a few days later.

The other 3 positions were never updated.

Then yesterday you wrote “My only open position is the march expo put.”

So you clearly closed the other 3 out, I would assume at a loss given price action.

If you were very clear and adamant about not taking longer term positions, even into Feb 28th, then it should be communicated by the actions you take with your money on intermediate termed positions you have already opened.

If not, then it leads to confusion as one might think you are still holding those positions.

Again, I understand that things change very fast, especially in this environment, and you seem to have a very open and creative personality which I think is awesome and that probably makes you a great empathetic teacher.

I also understand that English is your second language, and you are writing about a very complex subject outside your native language, and doing a great job at it. 

But, these variables can lead to a lot of confusion interpreting where you are at and what your dynamics of the market are. Or even what you are saying at times.

Two days this week I asked you to help clarify two very basic points and I couldn’t get a clear answer to the question, or it just goes completely ignored.

This is probably because you drop intraday post 10-20 minutes before the market opens, and by the time questions are asked about it, you are busy trading and setting up your positions as the data changes and the morning power hour begins.

Also, you are trying to rush getting the post out, writing it in a second language, trying to beat the clock before the opening bell, which can also lead to unclear language in your reports.

Not trying to be a dick, as your trades yesterday were incredible and deserve celebrating.

BUT, in the spirit of transparency and accuracy, it would be helpful if you also update on your other positions and report your losers. Especially when your outlook changes dramatically regarding March OPEX.

comment.body:
this is why i think i will close that thread, or at least clear it fully, and only give intraday signals when I trade, and on significant inflection points, bcs i just not have the capacity to post everything i do, or my algos do. 
it would be way easier to make via api, but tbh i never planned to give trading signals as written on twitter. and i know that in this format this is just a mess. 
ADI was closed in profit, the others were closed or stopped out on friday this is why i was done with open positions. 
if i remember correctly, i mentioned this in the daily chat maybe, but i only said about the call was closed with loss.
to not take longer-term positions was said in the posts, signaling that i don't see edge on holding longer-term positions. however note that sometimes traders chose 3-4dtes only bcs of a better expected payoff. 
plp asked me then when i still took those longs on nvda, aapl etc. that why i did so depsite what i wrote, and i said it is for short-term trade... just like an intraday trade. 
So yeah, i understand where the confusion lies, totally. and this was the original reason why i didn't want to give trading signals at all only analysis. 
...
if you don't get answer from me, tag me then. i have a lot of things to do beside this, i'm not full-time subscripe content provider, its like 30% of my time, including coding, thinking and writing the posts + deal with the stripe issues daily, letters and private messages too. so i can miss some messages.
i need to re-organize things in my life, bcs i don't like it this way either. the issue with daily post is always about the data providing, bcs i want the most fresh data i can take. 
...
my outlook didn't changed dramatically, you just didn't followed me ;))
